channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: This is THE Way to Build Custom AI Agents in 2025—Full Demo + Prompt Tips
executive_summary: In 2025, Notion released a powerful custom AI agent builder that is more advanced than many realize. This tool allows users to create tailored AI agents that can automate workflows, utilizing databases and connectors effectively. Mastering prompt engineering is crucial for leveraging these agents, as they can significantly enhance productivity but are also delicate to manage.
bullets: Notion's AI agents are described as 'the first truly accessible custom agent tool builder' available, which means they can be used by anyone, not just tech experts. This accessibility is important because it democratizes AI development, allowing more people to create solutions. Think of it like opening a bakery where anyone can learn to bake, not just professional chefs.

The video emphasizes that accurate prompting is key to making Notion's AI agents reliable. This means that how you ask the AI to perform tasks directly impacts its effectiveness. It's like giving a GPS a clear destination; if you don't specify where to go, it won't get you there.

Eight specific prompting rules are highlighted as essential for ensuring Notion's AI agents work properly. These rules guide users on how to phrase their requests to the AI, which is crucial for achieving desired outcomes. Think of these rules as the instructions for assembling a complex piece of furniture — without them, you might end up with a wobbly chair instead of a sturdy one.

The integration of databases, connectors, and a Multi-Channel Pipeline (MCP) is what unlocks automation in Notion. This combination allows for seamless data flow and task execution across different platforms. Imagine it like a well-oiled machine where each part works together to produce a smooth operation.

Notion's AI agents are described as 'powerful but fragile,' meaning they can deliver great results but require careful handling. Teams that learn to control and engineer prompts will gain significant advantages, while those who don't may face chaotic workflows. It's like having a high-performance sports car; if you don't know how to drive it, you risk crashing.

Operators can transform artifacts into agent-powered systems, which means they can take existing data or tools and enhance them with AI capabilities. This transformation is vital for maximizing efficiency and productivity in workflows. Think of it like upgrading an old computer with new software to make it run faster and better.

The video suggests that teams who master prompt engineering will see real leverage in their workflows. This means that understanding how to communicate with AI effectively can lead to significant improvements in output and efficiency. It's like learning to communicate in a new language; the better you get, the more opportunities open up.

Notion's AI tools are positioned as just another AI release, but they are much more powerful than that. This misperception may lead teams to underestimate their potential, which can hinder innovation. It's like seeing a plain box and not realizing it contains a treasure chest inside.

The speaker notes that implementation costs for AI tools are 'going to zero,' which means that adopting these technologies will become increasingly affordable. This trend is important for businesses looking to integrate AI without breaking the bank. Think of it like the price of smartphones dropping over time, making them accessible to everyone.

The video indicates that teams currently have a split of 80% focus on functionality and only 20% on hygiene in their evaluation tests. This imbalance can lead to messy code and inefficient systems. It's like a restaurant focusing solely on the food while neglecting cleanliness — a recipe for disaster.

By the end of the year, teams should aim to have at least 50% of their evaluation tests checking for code hygiene. This shift will help ensure that their systems are not only functional but also maintainable and scalable. Think of it like a gardener who not only plants flowers but also weeds the garden to keep it healthy.

The speaker encourages subscribing for daily AI strategy and news updates, which can help teams stay informed about the latest developments. Keeping up with trends is crucial in the fast-paced world of AI, as it can provide a competitive edge. It's like reading the morning news to stay updated on world events — knowledge is power.
key_takeaways: Implement a rule that at least 50% of your evaluation tests must check for code hygiene, not just functionality. This will help maintain clean and efficient code, preventing future issues. If you ignore this, you risk creating a system that is difficult to manage and scale.

Focus on mastering the eight prompting rules for Notion's AI agents. This knowledge is essential for ensuring that your AI tools work effectively and deliver the results you need. Without this understanding, you may end up with unreliable workflows that waste time and resources.

By the end of the year, ensure your team has integrated Notion's AI tools into at least one workflow. This will help you stay competitive and take advantage of the powerful capabilities these tools offer. Delaying this integration could mean falling behind in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

technical_terms.Custom AI Agent Builder: A tool that allows users to create personalized AI agents tailored to specific tasks or workflows. Think of it like a tailor who customizes a suit to fit you perfectly.
technical_terms.Prompt Engineering: The practice of crafting specific and effective requests to communicate with AI systems. Like giving clear directions to a taxi driver to ensure you arrive at your desired destination.
technical_terms.Multi-Channel Pipeline (MCP): A system that facilitates the flow of data and tasks across multiple platforms or channels. Think of it like a highway system that connects different cities, allowing for smooth travel between them.
technical_terms.Databases: Organized collections of data that can be easily accessed and managed. Like a library where books are categorized and can be quickly found.
technical_terms.Connectors: Tools that link different applications or systems together, allowing them to communicate and share data. Like bridges that connect two islands, enabling travel between them.
technical_terms.Artifacts: Existing data or tools that can be enhanced or transformed using AI capabilities. Think of it like an old painting that can be restored and made more vibrant with modern techniques.
technical_terms.Automation: The use of technology to perform tasks without human intervention. Like a robot vacuum that cleans your floors while you relax.
technical_terms.Code Hygiene: The practice of maintaining clean, organized, and efficient code in software development. Like keeping your kitchen clean while cooking to avoid a messy disaster.
technical_terms.Functionality: The ability of a system or tool to perform its intended tasks effectively. Think of it like a car that runs smoothly and gets you where you need to go.
technical_terms.Evaluation Tests: Tests designed to assess the performance and quality of software or systems. Like a fitness test that measures your strength and endurance.
